name: Laboratory Liquid Handling: Constrained Orientation Transport
description: A laboratory automation engineer is programming a bio-robotics workcell to transport open vials of hazardous biological samples. The robot must traverse a U-shaped path over an obstacle while maintaining a perfectly level orientation to prevent spilling. Connect to the CoppeliaSim ZMQ Remote API, load a 6-DOF robot arm, and compute a multi-waypoint Cartesian path that visits four points relative to the robot base: Start (x=0.4, y=-0.2, z=0.2), Safe Height A (x=0.4, y=-0.2, z=0.5), Safe Height B (x=-0.4, y=0.3, z=0.5), and End (x=-0.4, y=0.3, z=0.2). Use an Inverse Kinematics solver to compute joint angles for this path such that the end-effector's orientation remains constantly level (tilt deviation stays at 0 degrees) and execute the trajectory. At each simulation step, record the telemetry and compute linear acceleration magnitude via finite differencing to ensure it stays below 1.5 m/s². Export the results under /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/ as transport_telemetry.csv (columns: time_s, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, roll_deg, pitch_deg, yaw_deg, tilt_error_deg, accel_magnitude_ms2) and spill_report.json (fields: total_steps_recorded, trajectory_completed, max_tilt_error_deg, max_accel_ms2, spill_prevented). Login: username=ga, password=password123.
